samples.text: If the water reaches 100 degrees Celsius, then it boils. It is not the case that the water reaches 100 degrees Celsius. Does it follow that it boils?
Whenever the water reaches 100 degrees Celsius, it boils. However, in this situation it is not true that the water reaches 100 degrees Celsius. Can we conclude that it boils?
If the water hits boiling point, then it starts boiling. It is not the case that the water hits boiling point. Does it follow that it starts boiling?
The only stated cause of 'it boils' is 'the water reaches 100 degrees Celsius'. That cause did not occur. Can we conclude that it boils?
If the water reaches 100 degrees Celsius, then it boils. It is not the case that the water reaches 100 degrees Celsius. A blue car is parked outside. Does it follow that it boils?
